data.title: Commercial National Financial announces up to $1M stock buybacks program
data.description:
Bank holding company Commercial National Financial (OTCPK:CEFC) announced on Thursday the authorization of an up to $1M share repurchase program.

The company said the authorization remains in effect indefinitely.

Additionally, Commercial National Financial declared a $0.14/share quarterly dividend, in line with the previous.

data.title: GSK wins FDA label expansion for asthma therapy Nucala in COPD
data.description:
GSK (NYSE:GSK) announced Thursday that the U.S. FDA has further broadened the label for its asthma therapy, approving the injection as an add-on treatment for certain adults with chronic obstructive pulmonary disease (COPD).

Accordingly, the interleukin-5-targeting antibody therapy will therefore be available as an add-on maintenance treatment in the U.S. specifically for adults with inadequately controlled COPD and an eosinophilic phenotype, the British drugmaker said.

The approval is backed by data from the company’s MATINEE and METREX Phase 3 trials, which indicated a clinically meaningful and statistically significant decline in moderate/severe disease exacerbations versus placebo in COPD patients with an eosinophilic phenotype.

The FDA initially approved Nucala in 2015 as an add-on treatment for asthma in patients aged 12 years and older. Subsequently, the agency expanded its use for inflammatory conditions such as chronic rhinosinusitis with nasal polyps and Churg-Strauss syndrome.

data.title: Meritage Homes declares $0.43 dividend
data.description:
• Meritage Homes (NYSE:MTH) declared $0.43/share quarterly dividend, in line with previous.
  • Forward yield 2.66%
  • Payable June 30; for shareholders of record June 16; ex-div June 16.
  • See MTH Dividend Scorecard, Yield Chart, & Dividend Growth.

data.title: Amazon gains after Bill Ackman's Pershing Square discloses a new stake
data.description:
Amazon (NASDAQ:AMZN) gained in afternoon trading after Pershing Square Holdings (OTCPK:PSHZF) disclosed during an investor event that it holds a new position in the e-commerce stock. 

Pershing Square Chief Investment Officer Ryan Israel pointed to the strategic value of Amazon's (NASDAQ:AMZN) dual business model, particularly AWS with its high-growth potential. Overall, the firm sees the valuation of Amazon (AMZN) as attractive and thinks CEO Andy Jassy is doing an "incredible job" in managing macroeconomic headwinds.

The size of the Bill Ackman-led company's position in Amazon (AMZN) was not disclosed.

Shares of Amazon (AMZN) were up 1.6% in afternoon trading and are now up almost 20% over the last six weeks.

data.title: Bruker cut to Neural at Citi on potential order volatility
data.description:
Citi downgraded Bruker Corporation (NASDAQ:BRKR) to Neutral from Buy on Thursday, citing longer-than-expected volatility for the Billerica, Massachusetts-based life sciences firm’s orders amid federal funding cuts to science research.

Announcing its Q1 2025 results early this month, Bruker (NASDAQ:BRKR) cited a 20%–25% decline in its U.S. revenue from academic and government research customers due to research funding policy changes under the Trump administration.

“The company’s exposure to the US A&G market may lead to order volatility, which we believe may linger into 2026,” Bloomberg News reported Citi analyst Patrick Donnelly as saying in a research note.

“While BRKR’s (20%)-(25%) implied decline seems reasonable, we are more cautious on forward years,” the analyst added, with a $40 per share target on the stock.

BRKR’s trading multiple reflects some of these concerns, Donnelly wrote, adding that, however, Citi doesn’t foresee an opportunity for multiple expansion given these headwinds.

data.title: Roche’s Susvimo approved by FDA for diabetic retinopathy
data.description:
• Roche (OTCQX:RHHBY) announced that the U.S. FDA has approved Susvimo 100 mg/mL for the treatment of diabetic retinopathy.
  • The approval allows U.S. retina specialists to offer Susvimo to DR patients who have previously responded to at least two anti-VEGF injections.
  • The decision was supported by positive one-year data from Roche’s Phase III Pavilion study.
  • Susvimo is now available to eligible patients in the U.S., the company said.

data.title: Cybersecurity stocks in focus as BTIG says recent checks largely positive
data.description:
Cybersecurity stocks were in focus on Thursday as investment firm BTIG said recent channels checks into the space were largely positive.

Regarding CrowdStrike (NASDAQ:CRWD), six out of seven checks indicated demand in the first-quarter of 2025 was strong, and that they were either above plan or provided “positive directional commentary,” analyst Gray Powell wrote in a note to clients. Powell kept his Buy rating on CrowdStrike and raised his price target to $520 from $431.

For Okta (NASDAQ:OKTA), Powell said he had three discussions in the last few weeks and the feedback was constructive.

“Most notably, interest is growing the company’s [Identity & Access Governance] offering,” Powell wrote. “And OKTA is now seen as having completely recovered from the Oct '23 security incident.”

He kept his Buy rating and upped his price target to $142 from $123. 

Zscaler (NASDAQ:ZS) also saw positive feedback, as 10 contacts provided Powell with positive discussions and demand commentary. Eight of nine said they saw “strong” growth in the April quarter, and partners were generally at or above their targets. “We left our work feeling more confident in forecasts,” Powell wrote.

On the flip side, feedback for SentinelOne (NYSE:S) was slightly less positive, Powell said. Four of five checks said demand was “stable,” while only said they saw a slight improvement.

“While encouraging, we continue to hear about competitive concerns against CRWD and MSFT,” Powell explained.

data.title: Tinder CEO resigns as parent Match Group cuts jobs
data.description:
The top boss of dating app Tinder, Faye Iosotaluno, is stepping down, according to an official post on Thursday.

The move comes after Match Group's (NASDAQ:MTCH) new CEO, Spencer Rascoff, announced a 13% workforce trim to "reduce duplication and unlock scale" at the company, as it struggled to maintain payer growth on its family of apps.

"After almost 8 unforgettable years at Match Group and Tinder, I’ve made the incredibly hard decision to step down as Tinder’s CEO in July," Iosotaluno wrote on her LinkedIn account.

As part of cost-cutting efforts, certain key functions will be centralized, which will save more than $100M annually, the company said earlier this month.

In Q1, Match saw revenue shrink across its dating apps except Hinge. Payers fell 5% to 14.2M over the prior year quarter, driven by declines in Tinder and E&E.

data.title: Speculation begins on Rio Tinto's new CEO as Stausholm set to depart later this year
data.description:
Rio Tinto (NYSE:RIO) -0.6% in Thursday's trading after surprising investors with its announcement that CEO Jakob Stausholm will step down later this year once a successor is appointed, and giving no reason for the move.

Rio's (NYSE:RIO) board held meetings on the succession in recent months with the help of executive recruitment firm MWM Consulting, Reuters reported, vetting internal candidates including Bold Baatar, Simon Trott and Jerome Pecresse, while also looking for external leaders.

Chief commercial officer Baatar looks like the most likely successor, RBC Capital analysts said, believing his "communication, portfolio knowledge and problem-solving skills, as showcased during his role at Oyu Tolgoi and Simandou, would prove key in leading Rio,” referring to the company's projects to mine copper in Mongolia and iron ore in Guinea.

Baatar, a Mongolian, joined Rio (RIO) in 2013 and has worked in leadership positions in the company's marine, iron ore sales and marketing divisions; he joined the Executive Committee in 2016, running the Energy & Minerals product group, before heading its copper division.

Trott, the head of iron ore and a more than 20-year veteran at Rio (RIO), is building out a huge program of replacement tons, but some investors say the quality of ore in Rio's exports has dropped during his tenure, and the company has fallen short of production targets.

Pecresse was appointed head of the aluminium division in October 2023, joining the company from General Electric Renewable Energy, and previously worked at Alstom and Imerys.

Outside contenders are said to include Newmont (NEM) CEO Tom Palmer, who also was considered in 2020, and previous OZ Minerals CEO Andrew Cole, both former Rio veterans.

Rio (RIO) could recruit a dealmaker as its next CEO, as the conservative approach toward acquisitions of the departing Stausholm might have played a role in his exit, and archrival BHP's attempt to buy Anglo American last year raised the question of why Rio was not pursuing big deals, AJ Bell analyst Russ Mould said.

data.title: Arcutis wins FDA nod for Zoryve foam for plaque psoriasis
data.description:
• Arcutis Biotherapeutics (NASDAQ:ARQT) announced Thursday that the U.S. FDA approved a topical foam version of its plaque psoriasis therapy Zoryve (roflumilast) at 0.3% to treat scalp and body conditions associated with the skin disorder.
  • The decision related to the company’s supplemental New Drug Application ((sNDA)) targeted at FDA approval for Zoryve 0.3% to treat scalp and body lesions in patients aged 12 years and above with plaque psoriasis.
  • Previously, the company had regulatory clearance in the U.S. to market the once-daily therapy for a skin disorder known as seborrheic dermatitis in those aged nine years and older.
  • The decision was based on the company’s Phase 2 and pivotal Phase 3 trials designed to evaluate the anti-inflammatory agent in plaque psoriasis, and it marked the fifth FDA approval for Zoryve in less than three years.
